issues.tradition_claim: The Self is durvijñeya (hard to know); even hearing about it does not produce knowing of it. The verse acknowledges the asymmetry between exposure (paśyati / vadati / śṛṇoti) and understanding (veda).
issues.engineering_claim: The deepest part of any production system is composite tacit knowledge ... not extractable. ... Knowing is the third year on the rotation, not the second week.
issues.explanation: The engineering layer rescopes from atman-knowledge to system-tacit-knowledge. The argumentative shape is preserved — the gap between exposure and knowing is precisely the chain Shankara and Ramanuja both surface. The user's design note (2.29 is the most engineering-resonant verse) authorizes this rescoping in particular; no weakening, no inversion. The minor over-translation flagged by the sanskrit-scholar persona (cannot vs. does not) is documented.
